_comment: CONFIG 1 -- cloud-backed zarr peak browser (ATAC + RNA). Both datasets stream from the public S3 bucket via anndata read_lazy (backed_mode=true): obs/var/obsm load into memory, the expression matrix stays remote and is fetched one column at a time. PBMC_ATAC's var_names are peak coordinates (chr:start-end) so the matrix-backed ATAC Browser works directly; genome=hg38 auto-downloads the GENCODE v50 basic GTF for the gene-model track (cached once). Run: GUANACO_CONFIG=this.json guanaco -c this.json  (or set Main file for gunicorn). If your cluster has no internet, replace the sc_data URLs with local .zarr paths.

PBMC_RNA.description: Human PBMC scRNA-seq (RNA modality of a 10x multiome dataset), 8,677 cells x 36,591 genes, 7 cell types. Served remote-backed: X (CSC) stays on S3, gene columns are streamed on demand.

PBMC_ATAC.description: Matching PBMC scATAC-seq, 8,677 cells x 87,424 peaks (var_names are chr:start-end peak coordinates), 7 cell types. Served remote-backed; the ATAC Browser reads peak columns on demand and overlays GENCODE gene models.

title: GUANACO PBMC cloud peak browser -- RNA + ATAC (remote-backed zarr)
